title: Intense Algerian Flaxseed Tagine
description: A charred, shattering solo dinner that blends smoky Algerian spices with earthy flaxseed and savory dashi broth.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the beef cubes and sear on all sides until dark brown charred crust forms, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, and a pinch of salt over the meat, stirring to coat evenly.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic, cooking until the onion softens and becomes translucent.
Stir in the diced tomato, letting it break down into a thick sauce for 2 minutes.
Pour in the dashi broth, raise heat, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until the beef is tender.
Stir in the flaxseed, letting it absorb the sauce and swell slightly, then simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice, adjust seasoning, and serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs.
